response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned polyglot engineer with 16+ years of broad experience and genuine recent exposure to agentic tooling — including MCP server development and agentic workflow infrastructure at his current role. However, the resume reveals critical gaps against the Staff Forward Deployed Engineer requirements: Python is entirely absent from his skills list, which is a foundational language for the GenAI stack, and there is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. His AI exposure appears shallow and recent, limited to a single line in the current role description. The position demands 8+ years of production-grade AI delivery and demonstrable enterprise customer-facing technical leadership, neither of which is evidenced.
strengths: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server development and agentic workflow infrastructure at current role directly aligns with a key technology listed in the job description
Long-tenured staff-level contributor with demonstrated knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and conference talks, indicating mentorship capability
Data integration background at Dunnhumby (high-throughput microservices, real-time pipelines at millions of requests per second) shows relevant data engineering fundamentals
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills section and the entire resume — a non-negotiable for production GenAI engineering at this level
No mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation, or observability frameworks anywhere in the resume
No evidence of customer-facing or forward-deployed consulting work; the role requires leading enterprise technical discovery sessions with external stakeholders

key_factors.factor: No RAG or vector DB experience
